proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_enzalutamide'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide-treated patients had a response rate of 36.1% (n=20,076) vs 15.9% off-treatment (n=29,924); absolute difference +20.2 percentage points (chi-square p<1e-300).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_abiraterone'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone-treated patients had a response rate of 23.8% (n=14,991) vs 24.1% off-treatment (n=35,009); difference -0.3 pp, chi-square p=0.47.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_docetaxel'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Docetaxel-treated patients had a response rate of 24.0% (n=15,187) vs 24.0% off-treatment (n=34,813); difference -0.0 pp, chi-square p=0.91.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_olaparib'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib-treated patients had a response rate of 23.9% (n=5,098) vs 24.0% off-treatment (n=44,902); difference -0.1 pp, chi-square p=0.89.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_lu177_psma'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Lu177-PSMA-treated patients had a response rate of 24.3% (n=7,504) vs 24.0% off-treatment (n=42,496); difference +0.3 pp, chi-square p=0.60.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['treatment_pembrolizumab'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab-treated patients had a response rate of 23.9% (n=2,384) vs 24.0% off-treatment (n=47,616); difference -0.1 pp, chi-square p=0.88.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brca2_mutation have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without brca2_mutation.
Patients with ar_v7_positive have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without ar_v7_positive.
Patients with msi_high status have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without msi_high status.
Patients with psma_high status have a different rate of objective_response than patients without psma_high status.
Patients with mcrpc have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without mcrpc.
Patients with visceral_mets have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without visceral_mets.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['brca2_mutation'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-mutated 15.0% response (n=4,996) vs 25.0% in BRCA2-wild-type (n=45,004); diff -10.1 pp, chi-square p=5.4e-56.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['ar_v7_positive'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7+ 16.0% response (n=10,038) vs 26.0% in AR-V7- (n=39,962); diff -10.1 pp, chi-square p=1.9e-98.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['msi_high'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high 17.6% response (n=1,528) vs 24.2% MSI-low (n=48,472); diff -6.6 pp, chi-square p=3.0e-9.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['psma_high'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high 23.9% response (n=29,962) vs 24.3% PSMA-low (n=20,038); diff -0.4 pp, chi-square p=0.31. No main effect.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['mcrpc'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC 15.4% response (n=27,481) vs 34.6% non-mCRPC (n=22,519); diff -19.2 pp, chi-square p<1e-300.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['visceral_mets'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Visceral-mets 24.0% response (n=9,973) vs 24.0% no visceral mets (n=40,027); diff -0.1 pp, chi-square p=0.87.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher age_years is associated with a different rate of objective_response.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of ecog_ps on objective_response; rates by level: ECOG 0=28.2%, 1=22.8%, 2=18.2%.
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic decrease in response rate with rising ECOG (28.2% → 22.8% → 18.2%); univariate logistic coef per unit ECOG = -0.31 (z<<-10, p<<0.001).

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of gleason_score on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates flat across Gleason 6-10 (24.4%, 23.7%, 24.2%, 24.2%, 24.2%); univariate logistic p≈0.4. No meaningful main effect.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of age_years on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coef on age_years = +0.0002, p=0.82. No association.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of psa_ng_ml on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coef on psa_ng_ml = -0.0047 per ng/mL, z=-21, p=1.6e-94. Higher PSA strongly associated with lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of albumin_g_dl on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Albumin coef=+0.096 per g/dL, z=4.6, p=4.4e-6. Higher albumin → higher response.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of ldh_u_l on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: LDH coef=-1.0e-4 per U/L, p=0.68. No univariate association.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of weight_loss_pct_6mo on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Weight loss coef=-0.032 per 1 pp loss, z=-11, p=3.4e-29.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of crp_mg_l on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: CRP coef=-0.0069 per mg/L, z=-5.5, p=5.6e-8.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of nlr on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: NLR coef=-0.003 per unit, p=0.61. Univariate not significant.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of hemoglobin_g_dl on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: Hemoglobin coef=-0.004 per g/dL, p=0.51. No univariate association.

analyses.code: Univariate logistic of alkaline_phosphatase_u_l on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: ALP coef=+2e-4 per U/L, p=0.34. No univariate association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for prognostic features in a multivariable logistic regression, treatment_enzalutamide is independently associated with higher objective_response while the other 5 treatments are not.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response, [const + 31 features incl. all treatments]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic (n=50,000, pseudo-R²=0.124): treatment_enzalutamide coef=+1.20 (p<1e-300); abiraterone -0.027 (p=0.28); docetaxel +0.001 (p=0.96); olaparib +0.026 (p=0.49); lu177_psma +0.032 (p=0.31); pembrolizumab +0.006 (p=0.90). Independently negative: mcrpc -1.12, brca2 -0.71, ar_v7 -0.73, msi_high -0.23, ecog_ps -0.32, weight_loss -0.037, crp -0.007, psa -0.001. Independently positive: albumin +0.12.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in mcrpc patients than in non-mcrpc patients (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × mcrpc interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Non-mCRPC (n=22,519): enz response 61.0% vs 16.9% off, diff +44.0 pp. mCRPC (n=27,481): enz 15.8% vs 15.0% off, diff +0.8 pp (NS, p=0.09). Interaction logistic coef = -1.978, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive patients than in ar_v7_negative patients (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation carriers than in non-carriers (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × brca2_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-wild-type (n=45,004): enz 38.5% vs 16.0% off, diff +22.5 pp. BRCA2+ (n=4,996): enz 15.3% vs 14.7% off, diff +0.6 pp (p=0.57). Interaction coef=-1.138, p=3.3e-42.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in msi_high patients than in msi-low patients (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × msi_high interaction.
analyses.result_summary: MSI-low (n=48,472): enz 36.7% vs 15.8% off, diff +20.9 pp. MSI-high (n=1,528): enz 16.2% vs 18.4% off, diff -2.2 pp (p=0.30). Interaction coef=-1.283, p=1.8e-19.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is modified by psma_high status.
The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is modified by visceral_mets.
The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller in patients with higher psa_ng_ml (negative continuous interaction).

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × psma_high interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified diff: PSMA-low +20.0 pp, PSMA-high +20.4 pp. Interaction coef=+0.037, p=0.40. NOT a modifier.

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × visceral_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified diff: no visceral +20.3 pp, visceral +20.0 pp. Interaction coef=-0.009, p=0.86. NOT a modifier.

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_enzalutamide × psa_ng_ml interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=-0.0087 per ng/mL, p=1.2e-72. Higher baseline PSA blunts enzalutamide effect — collinear with mCRPC, which strongly inflates PSA.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib has a larger objective_response benefit in brca2_mutation carriers than in non-carriers (positive interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab has a larger objective_response benefit in msi_high patients than in msi-low patients (positive interaction).
treatment_lu177_psma has a larger objective_response benefit in psma_high patients than in psma-low patients (positive interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-WT: olaparib 25.3% vs 25.0% off, diff +0.3 pp (NS). BRCA2+: olaparib 12.1% (n=529) vs 15.3% off (n=4,467), diff -3.2 pp, p=0.058. Interaction coef=-0.289, p=0.045 — DIRECTIONALLY OPPOSITE expectation; olaparib does NOT benefit BRCA2 carriers in this dataset.

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction.
analyses.result_summary: MSI-low: pembro 24.1% vs 24.2% off, diff -0.2 pp. MSI-high (n=1,528): pembro 17.7% (n=79) vs 17.6% off (n=1,449), diff +0.1 pp (NS, p=1.0). Interaction coef=+0.017, p=0.96. Pembrolizumab does NOT benefit MSI-high subgroup despite biological rationale.

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high interaction.
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-low: lu177 25.0% vs 24.1% off, diff +0.9 pp. PSMA-high: lu177 23.8% vs 23.9% off, diff -0.1 pp. Interaction coef=-0.054, p=0.36. Lu177-PSMA does NOT preferentially benefit PSMA-high patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_lu177_psma's objective_response effect is smaller (or harmful) in visceral_mets patients than in non-visceral patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab's objective_response effect is smaller (or harmful) in visceral_mets patients than in non-visceral patients.

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_lu177_psma × visceral_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Non-visceral: lu177 24.9% vs 23.9% off, diff +1.0 pp (p=0.10). Visceral: lu177 21.9% (n=1,539) vs 24.3% off (n=8,434), diff -2.4 pp (p=0.04). Interaction coef=-0.191, p=0.010. Marginal heterogeneity but absolute effect sizes small in both directions.

analyses.code: Stratified rates and Logit with treatment_pembrolizumab × visceral_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Non-visceral: pembro 24.6% vs 24.0% off, diff +0.6 pp. Visceral: pembro 21.1% (n=494) vs 24.1% off (n=9,479), diff -3.1 pp (p=0.13). Interaction coef=-0.207, p=0.099. Trend only; not significant at α=0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After interaction screening of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab against all 6 binary biomarkers and 19 continuous features, none of these five treatments shows a robust effect-modifying covariate (no Bonferroni-significant interaction).

analyses.code: Loop: for tx in [abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab], for feature in [25 candidates], fit Logit(objective_response ~ tx + feature + tx*feature). Sort by p.
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values (all >0.01 after multiple-testing correction over ~125 tests): lu177_psma×visceral_mets p=0.010; docetaxel×sodium p=0.023; pembrolizumab×gleason p=0.033; olaparib×bun p=0.031; abiraterone×brca2 p=0.031. None survives Bonferroni (k≈125 → α*=4e-4). No robust effect modifier identified for the non-enzalutamide treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly larger absolute increase in objective_response than in the complement subgroup where any of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, or msi_high is present.

analyses.code: fav = (mcrpc==0)&(ar_v7_positive==0)&(brca2_mutation==0)&(msi_high==0); compare enz response in fav vs ~fav.
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (n=15,681): enz response 79.8% (n=6,325) vs 17.2% off (n=9,356), absolute diff +62.6 pp. Unfavorable subgroup (n=34,319): enz response 16.1% (n=13,751) vs 15.3% off (n=20,568), diff +0.8 pp (p=0.06). Massive concentration of treatment effect in joint-favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a logistic model with all four treatment_enzalutamide × biomarker interactions, each modifier (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high) independently and significantly reduces the enzalutamide log-odds effect, beyond the other three.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response ~ const + treatment_enzalutamide + mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high + 4 enz×modifier interactions)
analyses.result_summary: All four interactions remain massively significant when entered together: enz_x_mcrpc=-2.20 (p<1e-300), enz_x_arv7=-1.61 (p=1.5e-136), enz_x_brca2=-1.52 (p=2.0e-65), enz_x_msi=-1.65 (p=1.9e-27). Main treatment_enzalutamide coef=+2.58 (p<1e-300). Each modifier independently abolishes nearly all of the on-treatment log-odds advantage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Relaxing any one of the four favorable-subgroup constraints (allowing mcrpc, or ar_v7_positive, or brca2_mutation, or msi_high) substantially reduces the absolute treatment_enzalutamide benefit relative to the four-way joint-favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Compute enz absolute risk diff after individually dropping each constraint from the joint-favorable definition.
analyses.result_summary: Drops in absolute enz benefit when removing each constraint: drop mcrpc filter → diff falls from +62.6 pp to +28.7 pp; drop AR-V7 → +50.5 pp; drop BRCA2 → +56.3 pp; drop MSI → +60.7 pp. mCRPC contributes the largest single suppression of enzalutamide benefit; AR-V7 second; BRCA2 third; MSI smallest (because of low prevalence) but still abolishes effect inside its level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the four-way favorable subgroup, no remaining clinical, biomarker, or laboratory variable further significantly modifies the (already large) treatment_enzalutamide effect.

analyses.code: Within fav: for c in 21 candidate features, Logit(objective_response ~ enz + c + enz*c).
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, smallest interaction p-values: enz×psma_high (p=0.004; but stratified diffs almost equal: +64.8 pp PSMA-low vs +61.1 pp PSMA-high, so direction reflects scale not biology), enz×hemoglobin (p=0.058), enz×ecog_ps (p=0.069); none with material clinical magnitude. Conclude: no further modifier inside the joint-favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Concomitant administration of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT alter the absolute treatment_enzalutamide benefit within the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, stratify enz effect by each other-treatment status (0/1).
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, enzalutamide absolute response gain by concomitant agent: abiraterone 0/1 → +62.6 / +62.6 pp; docetaxel +62.9 / +61.8; olaparib +63.0 / +58.5; lu177-PSMA +62.2 / +64.6; pembrolizumab +62.4 / +65.8. No meaningful modulation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are non-mcrpc AND ar_v7_positive lose the enzalutamide effect almost entirely (suppression by AR-V7 is not contingent on mcrpc).

analyses.code: Cross-tab: enz response within each of the 4 cells of mcrpc × ar_v7_positive.
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC=0 / AR-V7=0 (n=18,000): enz 71.7% vs 17.1% off, diff +54.6 pp. mCRPC=0 / AR-V7+ (n=4,519): enz 17.7% vs 16.3% off, diff +1.4 pp. mCRPC=1 / AR-V7=0 (n=21,962): enz 15.9% vs 15.0%, diff +0.8 pp. mCRPC=1 / AR-V7+ (n=5,519): enz 15.5% vs 15.1%, diff +0.5 pp. Either suppressor alone is sufficient to abolish the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner random forest CATE for treatment_enzalutamide, trained on all 25 baseline features, will rank the four binary modifiers (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high) as the dominant drivers of treatment-effect heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Train RandomForestClassifier separately on enz==1 and enz==0 subsets; compute per-patient CATE = p1 - p0; rank features driving CATE via RandomForestRegressor on CATE.
analyses.result_summary: Top CATE feature importances: mcrpc 0.665, ar_v7_positive 0.205, brca2_mutation 0.099, msi_high 0.023, psa_ng_ml 0.004, ecog_ps 0.001 — exactly the four binary suppressors at the top, in the same order as the regression-based interaction effect sizes. CATE Q4 patients (n=12,500) are 100% non-mCRPC, 100% AR-V7-, 100% BRCA2-, 100% MSI-low; observed enz effect in Q4: +71.3 pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the unfavorable subgroup (any of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high present), adjusted treatment_enzalutamide effect on log-odds of objective_response is at most modestly positive (clinically negligible).

analyses.code: Within unfav (n=34,319): sm.Logit(objective_response ~ const + age + ecog + visceral + psa + gleason + brca2 + arv7 + msi + psma + alb + ldh + wt_loss + crp + nlr + treatment_enzalutamide).
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enzalutamide log-odds coef within unfavorable subgroup = +0.061 (p=0.046). Translates to ~1 pp absolute response increase — clinically irrelevant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is similar across ecog_ps strata in the overall cohort (constant absolute risk difference even though baseline response declines with ECOG).

analyses.code: Stratify enz response by ecog_ps level (0,1,2).
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0 (n=17,592): enz 40.6% vs 19.9% off, diff +20.7 pp. ECOG 1 (n=24,971): enz 34.8% vs 14.7% off, diff +20.1 pp. ECOG 2 (n=7,437): enz 30.1% vs 10.3% off, diff +19.8 pp. Absolute benefit nearly constant across ECOG; full-cohort interaction on log-odds scale (+0.14, p=1.6e-5) reflects ratio-scale heterogeneity, not absolute-scale modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Independent prognostic features in the multivariable logistic model (adjusted for treatments and other features) include: ecog_ps↓, mcrpc↓, ar_v7_positive↓, brca2_mutation↓, msi_high↓, weight_loss_pct_6mo↓, crp_mg_l↓, psa_ng_ml↓, albumin_g_dl↑.

analyses.code: Adjusted logistic with 31 covariates including all 6 treatments.
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted coefficients (sign on log-odds objective_response): ecog_ps -0.32 (p<1e-50); mcrpc -1.12; ar_v7 -0.73; brca2 -0.71; msi_high -0.23; weight_loss -0.037; crp -0.007; psa -0.001; albumin +0.12. Non-significant after adjustment: ldh, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, gleason_score, age_years, visceral_mets, nlr.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint-favorable subgroup, lu177_psma still does not benefit psma_high vs psma_low patients (no PSMA-high-specific signal preserved when other modifiers are removed).

analyses.code: Stratify lu177-PSMA effect within joint-favorable subgroup (n=15,681) by psma_high.
analyses.result_summary: Within joint-favorable + psma_high (n=9,404): lu177 response equivalent to off-treatment (no benefit). Within psma_high & non-visceral_mets (n=23,941, broader stratum): lu177 24.2% vs 23.8% off, diff +0.4 pp, p=0.65. The biological expectation that lu177-PSMA benefits PSMA-high patients is not realized in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete best-supported treatment-effect subgroup in this cohort is: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive effect on objective_response (≈+63 percentage points absolute, ≈80% on-treatment vs ≈17% off-treatment) ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this joint subgroup the absolute effect is ≤+1 pp and clinically negligible. None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab shows a meaningful effect on objective_response in any subgroup probed.

analyses.code: Confirmatory: 4-way joint subgroup test + adjusted within-strata logistic + RF CATE concordance.
analyses.result_summary: Joint-favorable subgroup (n=15,681; 31% of cohort): on-treatment response 79.8%, off-treatment 17.2%, absolute diff +62.6 pp, p<1e-300. Joint-unfavorable subgroup (n=34,319; 69% of cohort): on-treatment 16.1%, off-treatment 15.3%, diff +0.8 pp, adjusted log-odds coef +0.061. T-learner random-forest CATE Q4 (n=12,500) is 100% non-mCRPC AND AR-V7- AND BRCA2- AND MSI-low and has observed +71.3 pp benefit. All other treatments null in every subgroup checked.
